# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant the account 'roles/owner' at project level so it can manage all GCP resources without permission errors. This enables full automation of our infrastructure deployments.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources:
- name: 'projects/my_gcp_proj/*'
  type: projects/my_gcp_proj/.yaml
  properties:
    id: myProjectID # replace with your project ID here. This can be retrieved via `gcloud config get-value core/project` command in a gcloud script or programmatic call to GCP APIs using client libraries as per requirement language's standard library for the respective API (e.g., Google Cloud Identity Platform, etc).
    labels:  # add any custom tags here if necessary  
      - key: "env"    
        value: 'production', '-key': tag to give more context about this resource e..-value' production'. If you don’t want a particular label on the first time creation of service account then use null.  E, gcloud commands and scripts provide various options for creating resources or deploying them in YAML files like so: `gcloud -q projects add-iam-policy-binding $PROJECT_ID --member=serviceAccount:my-email@example.com--role roles/owner
